We know that the modelling, simulation, and optimisation tools in QruiseML don't exist in a vacuum. There are several established open-source tools already used quite widely by the quantum computing community. One of the key features of qruise-toolset is its compatibility with QuTiP which allows you to reuse code from your existing simulations and also voids the need to learn a new interface for defining your system models.

We know that the modelling, simulation, and optimisation tools in QruiseML don't exist in a vacuum. There are several established open-source tools already used quite widely by the quantum computing community. One of the key features of `qruise-toolset` is its compatibility with QuTiP which allows you to reuse code from your existing simulations and also voids the need to learn a new interface for defining your system models.
Besides ensuring compatibility with existing tools, we've also benchmarked the software against QuTiP and Dynamiqs, two of the most widely used libraries for scalable quantum dynamics simulation.
A lot of this comes from the underlying JAX library (also used by Dynamiqs).
We'll release more results in the coming months as we run benchmarks for other use-cases.

A broad class of quantum dynamics problems – Schrödinger, von Neumann and Lindbladian – can be efficiently solved using our extensive library of ODE and piecewise constant solvers. Under the hood, it is powered by JAX which provides support for scaling large ensemble problems on CPUs & GPUs and also gives us automatic differentiation, necessary for fast optimal control.

Today, Qruise announced the release of Resonance, a new software platform for modelling and enhancing magnetic resonance (MR) systems.
Its flagship platform, QruiseML, is used by R&D teams to design high-fidelity quantum gates to characterise systems across all major quantum computing platforms.
Resonance builds directly on this foundation, adapting these proven tools to the specific physics and hardware constraints of MR systems.

Saarbrücken, Germany | 29 April 2025 – Today, Qruise announced the release of [Resonance](https://www.qruise.com/products/qruise-mr), a new software platform for modelling and enhancing magnetic resonance (MR) systems.

Since its founding in 2021, Qruise has built a reputation for delivering advanced tools for quantum system modelling and control. Its flagship platform, QruiseML, is used by R&D teams to design high-fidelity quantum gates to characterise systems across all major quantum computing platforms.

"With today's launch of Resonance, we are unveiling the first milestone in Qruise's strategic roadmap: creating a general-purpose AI Physicist that can tackle any R&D field defined by challenging physics," said Shai Machnes, CEO of Qruise.

interaction_phrase: installed for internal use by the IQCC team
occurrence_sentence: The setup combines a 21-qubit QuantWare Contralto QPU with the OPX1000 control stack, enabling real-time, low-latency hybrid control, both fully integrated through QruiseOS and installed for internal use by the IQCC team for multiplexed bring-up operations.

Saarbrücken, Germany | Tuesday April 22nd, 2026 – Qruise has completed a proof-of-concept integration of its automated bring-up software, QruiseOS, at the Israeli Quantum Computing Center (IQCC), where Quantum Machines' control systems underpin key elements of the control infrastructure.

With this integration, Qruise and IQCC collaborated to explore automated calibration workflows of the Contralto QPU, including single-qubit bring-up followed by experiments such as CZ flux chevron, conditional phase and local phase optimisations, and interleaved randomised benchmarking.

interaction_phrase: control systems underpin key elements of the control infrastructure
occurrence_sentence: Qruise has completed a proof-of-concept integration of its automated bring-up software, QruiseOS, at the Israeli Quantum Computing Center (IQCC), where Quantum Machines' control systems underpin key elements of the control infrastructure.

interaction_phrase: Built on Quantum Machines' OPX1000 and Quantum Orchestration Platform (QOP)
occurrence_sentence: Built on Quantum Machines' OPX1000 and Quantum Orchestration Platform (QOP), this deployment supports automated bring-up workflows and advanced data management in a multi-user, multi-QPU environment.

interaction_phrase: installed for internal use by the IQCC team
occurrence_sentence: The setup combines a 21-qubit QuantWare Contralto QPU with the OPX1000 control stack, enabling real-time, low-latency hybrid control, both fully integrated through QruiseOS and installed for internal use by the IQCC team for multiplexed bring-up operations.

With this integration, Qruise and IQCC collaborated to explore automated calibration workflows of the Contralto QPU, including single-qubit bring-up followed by experiments such as CZ flux chevron, conditional phase and local phase optimisations, and interleaved randomised benchmarking.

interaction_phrase: integrated with the Qruise knowledge base
occurrence_sentence: Each experiment features robust, fault-tolerant analysis and is fully integrated with the Qruise knowledge base, an advanced data management and experiment-tracking platform.

interaction_phrase: provide a platform where companies like Qruise can develop and validate their software
occurrence_sentence: "As we expand the IQCC testbed, we are proud to provide a platform where companies like Qruise can develop and validate their software on real, uncalibrated quantum systems, while benefiting from the support of our expert team," said Nir Alfasi, General Manager at IQCC.
Quantum Machines emphasised the importance of integration-focused research in national testbeds as part of the broader transition toward hybrid quantum-classical architectures.
Qruise highlighted the operational value of automated workflows during ongoing testing.

Qruise to help build open architecture quantum testbed with £1.65M Innovate UK grant
Qruise is part of a consortium awarded £1.65 million by Innovate UK to develop an open architecture quantum (OAQ) testbed.

The project, developed in collaboration with TreQ, Rigetti, Oxford Ionics, and Q-CTRL, forms part of the UK Quantum Missions pilot, which aims to accelerate quantum technologies in line with the National Quantum Strategy.

occurrence_sentence: The broader testbed effort will be led by TreQ, who will host and maintain the system at its facility in Oxfordshire.
The initial focus will be on superconducting and trapped-ion systems, with Rigetti supplying its 9-qubit Novera™ QPU and Oxford Ionics contributing its trapped-ion technology.

interaction_phrase: introduced a revamped dashboard in QruiseOS
occurrence_sentence: This year, we introduced a revamped dashboard in QruiseOS, making it easy to track workflows, manage QPUs and users, and view historical data.
* [QruiseML: streamlined simulation & optimal control](https://www.qruise.com/news-and-events/news/2025-wrapup#qruiseml-streamlined-simulation--optimal-control)
This year, we introduced a revamped dashboard in QruiseOS, making it easy to track workflows, manage QPUs and users, and view historical data.
This year brought substantial improvements to qruise-toolset, the Python library underpinning QruiseML.

To help users find their way around, we've added lots more [docs pages](https://docs.qruise.com/latest/qruiseos/intro/), including user guides, knowledge base docs, flow file references, and initial setup guides.

interaction_phrase: substantial improvements to `qruise-toolset`, the Python library underpinning [QruiseML](https://www.qruise.com/products/qruise-ml)
occurrence_sentence: This year brought substantial improvements to `qruise-toolset`, the Python library underpinning [QruiseML](https://www.qruise.com/products/qruise-ml).

We also added a whole host of [example notebooks](https://docs.qruise.com/2025.11.0/qruiseml/intro/#example-notebooks) to make it easy to get started simulating and optimising a wide range of quantum technologies and control stacks.

We added five new sections to the QruiseOS documentation to help users at every step of their journey as they get familiarised with the software:
The entry point for all non-admin users of QruiseOS, this section covers things like setting up access tokens, running measurements, and navigating the dashboard and knowledge base.

This section provides step-by-step tutorials for common use-cases such as executing workflows and managing data, and is designed to be read alongside the corresponding example notebooks in the QruiseOS JupyterLab.
Characterisation data is stored in a structured format within the QruiseOS knowledge base.
These pages provide a comprehensive reference to the syntax used by the Qruise flow files and how you can design your own bring-up recipes based on the requirements of your quantum computing stack
We have fully revamped this feature to provide a unified interface for managing all your QPUs, users, and the specific level of access that each user has for each QPU.
There is also a single unified identity file for easy environment configuration.
You can learn how to do this step by step in our new [Configuring the environment](https://docs.qruise.com/latest/qruiseos/getting-started/api_token/) getting started guide.
Providing granular access control in multi-QPU, multi-user environments is one of the key features of QruiseOS.

interaction_phrase: packages these tools into an end-to-end solution
occurrence_sentence: QruiseMR packages these tools into an end-to-end solution that combines full system modelling, physics-based model learning, and advanced pulse-level optimisation to produce MR pulse sequences that have greater spectral and spatial selectivity and are more robust to fluctuations.

interaction_phrase: packages these tools into an end-to-end solution
occurrence_sentence: QruiseMR packages these tools into an end-to-end solution that combines full system modelling, physics-based model learning, and advanced pulse-level optimisation to produce MR pulse sequences that have greater spectral and spatial selectivity and are more robust to fluctuations.

QruiseMR packages these tools into an end-to-end solution that combines full system modelling, physics-based model learning, and advanced pulse-level optimisation to produce MR pulse sequences that have greater spectral and spatial selectivity and are more robust to fluctuations. ![robust-pulse](https://www.qruise.com/mri/robust-pulse.png) [Discover QruiseMR](https://www.qruise.com/products/qruise-mr)
High-fidelity digital twins with QruiseMR

QruiseMR packages these tools into an end-to-end solution that combines full system modelling, physics-based model learning, and advanced pulse-level optimisation to produce MR pulse sequences that have greater spectral and spatial selectivity and are more robust to fluctuations.
High-fidelity digital twins with QruiseMR

QruiseMR packages these tools into an end-to-end solution that combines full system modelling, physics-based model learning, and advanced pulse-level optimisation to produce MR pulse sequences that have greater spectral and spatial selectivity and are more robust to fluctuations. ![robust-pulse](https://www.qruise.com/mri/robust-pulse.png) [Discover QruiseMR](https://www.qruise.com/products/qruise-mr)

Qruise is a key member of the QuE-MRT project with the aim of "Revolutionising cancer imaging through quantum technologies". As part of the project, we've been developing highly selective and robust control pulse sequences that precisely target a single sample slice, minimising spin excitations in other slices for more accurate and efficient imaging.

On the 100-qubit level, we will team up with well-funded national initiatives and explore application-adapted strategies (co-designs) and grow existing high-quality fabrication capabilities to that scale.

QuRIOUS continues several earlier initiatives on optical clocks that were also supported by the European Commission; in particular the MoSaiQC training network that is currently training 16 doctoral students on optical atomic clocks, as well as the iqClock research collaboration and its successor, AQuRA, aimed at making optical quantum clocks ready for use in industry.

interaction_phrase: QruiseOS is a collection of algorithms
occurrence_sentence: Qruise (as in cruise control): The QruiseOS is a collection of algorithms – optimizers, simulators, machine learning tools and other software components designed to accelerate the development of quantum technology and to improve its application.

interaction_phrase: QruiseOS is a collection of algorithms
occurrence_sentence: Qruise (as in cruise control): The QruiseOS is a collection of algorithms – optimizers, simulators, machine learning tools and other software components designed to accelerate the development of quantum technology and to improve its application.

This includes software for characterization and calibration of the quantum computer for initial configuration and firmware for obtaining high-precision operation, the operating system for task management that ensures that QPU idle time is minimized, and the software stack that translates highly abstract algorithms into hardware-specific code. ![Affiliate Logo](https://www.qruise.com/cms/research-projects/qcstack/affiliations/fraunhofer.png)![Affiliate Logo](https://www.qruise.com/cms/research-projects/qcstack/affiliations/university-berlin.png)

Qruise extends NV centre bring-up through collaboration with Goethe University Frankfurt

Following its ambition to become completely hardware agnostic, Qruise is working with XeedQ and the Modular Supercomputing and Quantum Computing (MSQC) group at Goethe University Frankfurt to enable reliable operation of nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centre quantum systems.

Building on the bring-up results yielded by QruiseOS, Qruise utilised its simulation software, [QruiseML](https://qruise.com/products/qruise-ml), to build a differentiable digital twin of the entire system.
